channel.title: Photo Feeds
channel.description: Combined feed of recent photo essays from The Atlantic, The Guardian, The Boston Globe, NYT, and Reuters.

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] Merlin the duck and a Van Gogh pool: photos of the day – Tuesday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Gaza City, Gaza — An overcrowded makeshift camp set up along the Mediterranean coastline of Gaza, where the population are struggling to access basic necessities such as food, clean drinking water, electricity and essential healthcareShare. — Photograph: Ahmed Jihad Ibrahim Al-arini/Anadolu/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] Merlin the duck and a Van Gogh pool: photos of the day – Tuesday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Suzhou, China — Containers stacked at an illuminated container terminal in Zhangjiagang PortShare. — Photograph: Shi Bairong/VCG/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] Merlin the duck and a Van Gogh pool: photos of the day – Tuesday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
London, UK — The Lib Dem leader, Ed Davey, stands next to a billboard for a new campaign targeting the Reform leader, Nigel Farage, on the 10th anniversary of the Brexit referendumShare. — Photograph: Dan Kitwood/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] Merlin the duck and a Van Gogh pool: photos of the day – Tuesday

Chengdu, China — Swimmers in a pool featuring a mosaic of Vincent van Gogh’s The Starry NightShare. — Photograph: VCG/Getty Images

item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Zapopan, Mexico — Colombia fans gather for a banderazo (a large gathering of supporters) ahead of tomorrow’s Fifa World Cup match against Democratic Republic of the CongoShare. — Photograph: David Ramos/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] Merlin the duck and a Van Gogh pool: photos of the day – Tuesday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
La Paz, Bolivia — People shop for agricultural products after police driving bulldozers cleared highways, and fuel trucks entered the city following anti-government blockadesShare. — Photograph: Martin Bernetti/AFP/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

Cambridge, UK — Students cross Trinity Bridge as punts fill the River Cam after a firework display during the university’s Trinity May Ball, celebrating the end of the academic yearShare. — Photograph: Joe Giddens/PA

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

Paris, France — A man dives into the Canal Saint-Martin as temperatures rise during a heatwave affecting parts of EuropeShare. — Photograph: Abdul Saboor/Reuters

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] Merlin the duck and a Van Gogh pool: photos of the day – Tuesday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Van, Turkey — A visitor walks through the shallow waters of Akgöl, which usually dries up during the summer months but has refilled after heavy rainfallShare. — Photograph: Ozkan Bilgin/Anadolu/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] Merlin the duck and a Van Gogh pool: photos of the day – Tuesday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Mexico City, Mexico — Merlin the duck receives a kiss from Christian Gómez, son of the pet’s owner, in the National Palace after President Sheinbaum invited Merlin and his family to make him an official World Cup mascotShare. — Photograph: José Méndez/EPA

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] Merlin the duck and a Van Gogh pool: photos of the day – Tuesday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Maracay, Venezuela — Vets feed baby white lion cubs at Las Delicias zoo. The Timbavati subspecies are considered endangered and the global population barely exceeds 100Share. — Photograph: Jacinto Oliveros/AFP/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] California, Seen Through the Lens of George Rose
item.description:
George Rose / Getty — Heavy surf created by a large storm in the Gulf of Alaska sent huge waves crashing along the coastal rocks and shoreline near the Piedras Blancas Lighthouse on February 4, 2016.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] California, Seen Through the Lens of George Rose
item.description:
George Rose / Getty — A farmer riding a tractor cleans out weeds in an old-vine zinfandel vineyard near Healdsburg, California, on March 22, 2022.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] California, Seen Through the Lens of George Rose
item.description:
George Rose / Getty — A large statue of a Brontosaurus stands at the Cabazon Dinosaur roadside attraction off Interstate 10, seen on May 11, 2022.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] California, Seen Through the Lens of George Rose
item.description:
George Rose / Getty — A mixture of snow, rain, and sand moves across Death Valley on March 2, 2023, near Furnace Creek, California.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] California, Seen Through the Lens of George Rose
item.description:
George Rose / Getty — Tourists flock to patches of aspens, cottonwoods, and maple trees that turn bright red, yellow, and gold along Bishop Creek Canyon and in the canyons of North Lake, Lake Sabrina, and South Lake, near Bishop, California, on October 9, 2021.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] California, Seen Through the Lens of George Rose
item.description:
George Rose / Getty — A large grass-and-timber fire, the Lake Fire, dominates the horizon in Santa Barbara County’s Santa Ynez Valley, viewed from a nearby vineyard on July 6, 2024.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] California, Seen Through the Lens of George Rose
item.description:
George Rose / Getty — More than 58,000 fiber-optic spheres cover a rolling hillside and gully in a “made for social media” Field of Light at Sensorio on June 1, 2019, near Paso Robles.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] California, Seen Through the Lens of George Rose
item.description:
George Rose / Getty — Smoke from a controlled burn produces a haze above the Merced River, seen in the hours before sunrise on October 28, 2021, in Yosemite National Park.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] California, Seen Through the Lens of George Rose
item.description:
George Rose / Getty — The Sierra Nevada Mountains, viewed from the scenic Alabama Hills at sunrise on April 6, 2017, near Lone Pine. Owens Valley is an arid valley in eastern California, to the east of the Sierra Nevadas and west of the White Mountains and Inyo Mountains on the west edge of the Great Basin.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] California, Seen Through the Lens of George Rose
item.description:
George Rose / Getty — An In-N-Out Burger restaurant adjacent to a runway at Los Angeles International Airport has become a favorite Instagram spot to photograph jets as they land, seen on February 7, 2022, in Los Angeles.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] California, Seen Through the Lens of George Rose
item.description:
George Rose / Getty — A large oak tree is silhouetted against the early-morning sky as the sun comes up on December 26, 2021, near Santa Ynez, California, in Santa Barbara County’s wine country.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] California, Seen Through the Lens of George Rose
item.description:
George Rose / Getty — Lake Oroville, California’s second-largest water reservoir, stood at a historically low level during a drought, seen on September 1, 2021, near Oroville, California.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] California, Seen Through the Lens of George Rose
item.description:
George Rose / Getty — Elephant seals gather on the beach at the Piedras Blancas Elephant Seal Rookery on February 21, 2019, south of the Piedras Blancas Lighthouse and just north of San Simeon, California.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] California, Seen Through the Lens of George Rose
item.description:
George Rose / Getty — Trees stand along the Merced River, coated in white after a major storm dumped a foot of snow in Yosemite Valley on December 16, 2021, in Yosemite National Park.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] California, Seen Through the Lens of George Rose
item.description:
George Rose / Getty — Following record winter rains, colorful poppies and other wildflowers appeared in the Antelope Valley Poppy Preserve one hour north of Los Angeles, drawing visitors to a “super bloom” on March 31, 2019, near Lancaster, California.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] California, Seen Through the Lens of George Rose
item.description:
George Rose / Getty — Early-morning light shines on Emerald Bay in South Lake Tahoe, California, on October 26, 2022.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] California, Seen Through the Lens of George Rose
item.description:
George Rose / Getty — Visitors to Anza-Borrego Desert State Park take in Galleta Meadows, a sculpture garden by the artist and welder Ricardo Breceda, on March 6, 2019, in Borrego Springs, California.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] California, Seen Through the Lens of George Rose
item.description:
George Rose / Getty — A woman photographs the Golden Gate Bridge and approaching bank of heavy fog on February 13, 2014, in San Francisco.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] California, Seen Through the Lens of George Rose
item.description:
George Rose / Getty — Discovery Bay, a waterfront community of 15,000 people built on a network of man-made dikes and surrounded by fresh water, is viewed from the air on May 22, 2023, over Discovery Bay, California.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] California, Seen Through the Lens of George Rose
item.description:
George Rose / Getty — Pinot-noir vineyards in the famed Sta. Rita Hills AVA (American Viticultural Area) turn a vivid red, orange, and yellow, signaling the end of the grape harvest and approach of winter, as viewed on November 26, 2023, near Buellton, California.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] California, Seen Through the Lens of George Rose
item.description:
George Rose / Getty — A group of surfers catches the same wave in this photo taken from the pier in Pismo Beach, California, on August 21, 2015.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] California, Seen Through the Lens of George Rose
item.description:
George Rose / Getty — The downtown high-rises are viewed at sunrise from the Kenneth Hahn State Recreation Area in the Baldwin Hills Mountains on February 7, 2022, in Los Angeles.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] California, Seen Through the Lens of George Rose
item.description:
George Rose / Getty — A farmworker rides a horse through the neighborhood backstreets in Los Alamos, California, on January 30, 2016.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] California, Seen Through the Lens of George Rose
item.description:
George Rose / Getty — Heavy rains arrived in Santa Barbara County wine country, filling the creeks and streams and turning the hillsides a vivid green as viewed on January 20, 2023, near Santa Ynez, California. Following the notoriety from the Academy Award–winning film Sideways, this farming region north of Santa Barbara has become a popular wine-country stop for global and domestic tourists traveling between San Francisco and Los Angeles.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] California, Seen Through the Lens of George Rose
item.description:
George Rose / Getty — Yosemite Valley, including Half Dome, Clouds Rest, Bridalveil Fall, and El Capitan, viewed from Tunnel View after a brief rainstorm cleared on March 27, 2025, in Yosemite National Park, California

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Versailles, France — Donald Trump receives a tour of Chateau de Versailles from Emmanuel Macron ahead of a dinner following the conclusion of the G7 summit. Trump responded to criticism of his ceasefire deal with Iran, warning at the summit that he was prepared to go back to dropping bombs and insisting the deal did not require the US to pay even 10 cents to IranShare. — Photograph: Anna Moneymaker/Reuters

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Chicago, US — Sasha Obama, Malia Obama, Michelle Obama, Barack Obama, Bill Clinton and Hillary Clinton attend the dedication ceremony for the opening of the Barack Obama Presidential Center in John Lewis Plaza in Chicago. Featuring appearances by a cast of musical stars and retired politicians from a less polarised era, it was a seemingly perfect antidote to the spectacle of cage fights on the White House lawnShare. — Photograph: Win McNamee/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Washington, DC, US — Diego Lopes celebrates after defeating Steve Garcia in a featherweight bout during the UFC Freedom 250 mixed martial arts event on the South Lawn of the White House. Beneath a giant steel canopy known as the Claw, with military flyovers overhead and thousands of spectators spread across the South Lawn and nearby Ellipse, Donald Trump celebrated his 80th birthday and the forthcoming semiquincentennial by staging the first professional sporting event in White House historyShare. — Photograph: Chip Somodevilla/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Kansas City, US — Argentina’s Lionel Messi enters the pitch before the 2026 World Cup match against Algeria at the Kansas City Stadium. Messi scored a hat-trick to draw level with Germany’s Miroslav Klose for the World Cup’s all-time mark of 16 goalsShare. — Photograph: Roberto Schmidt/AFP/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Dallas, US — England’s Jude Bellingham celebrates with Harry Kane and Noni Madueke after scoring against Croatia in their World Cup match at Dallas Stadium. Bellingham was in the mood throughout the game, determined to play without fear and to the point of maximum expression, and it was the midfielder that tilted the contest in England’s favour with a storming run and finishShare. — Photograph: MI News/NurPhoto/Shutterstock

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
New York City, US — New York Knicks fans celebrate the team’s NBA championship during their first-ever paradeShare.

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Boborás, Spain — An active wildfire burns in the north-western Spanish region of Galicia as firefighters ctry to contain a blazeShare. — Photograph: Brais Lorenzo/Zuma/Shutterstock

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Bitlis, Turkey — Sheep herds are driven toward the summer pastures of Nemrut mountain from the village of KiyiduzuShare. — Photograph: Mustafa Kilic/Anadolu/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Mongbwalu, Democratic Republic of the Congo — Female miners, known locally as decalots, wear plastic bags over their clothing to protect themselves from mud and dirt as they sort out rocks containing gold particles at an artisanal mining site. A month on since the latest outbreak of Ebola was first identified in the DRC, the latest figures from the UN show 676 confirmed cases and 136 deathsShare. — Photograph: Jospin Mwisha/AFP/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
London, UK — A supporter of the banned protest group Palestine Action is arrested by police during a demonstration outside the high court in London. The home secretary’s decision to ban Palestine Action was lawful, the court of appeal has ruled. A five-strong panel, including the two most senior judges in England and Wales, overturned February’s decision of the high court that the proscription of the direct action group, the first to be banned under the Terrorism Act, was wrongShare. — Photograph: Neil Hall/EPA

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Geneva, Switzerland — A demonstrator throws branches in the fire of a burning trash container during a rally of a No-G7 coalition of more than 60 associations, unions and leftwing groups ‘against fascism and imperialism’, before the start of the G7 summit in Évian-les-BainsShare. — Photograph: Gabriel Monnet/AFP/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Kfar Yona, Israel — Israeli security forces stand guard as ultra-Orthodox Jews gather to protest against military conscription outside Beit Lid military prisonShare. — Photograph: Ilia Yefimovich/AFP/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Gaza — Zeina al-Habil hugs the body of her father after he and her five-year-old brother were killed in an Israeli missile strike that targeted their home in the neighbourhood of Sheikh Radwan. The International Committee of the Red Cross said this week the risk that the thousands of Palestinians buried beneath Gaza’s rubble may never be identified is increasing by the day, as recovery efforts remain slow and many victims have yet to be retrieved.Share. — Photograph: Omar al-Qattaa/AFP/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Tyre, Lebanon — A woman walks past rubble at the site of an Israeli strike. Talks due to take place on Friday between the US ⁠and ​Iran in Switzerland to implement a peace deal were cancelled as Hezbollah killed four Israeli soldiers and Israel carried out a wave of retaliatory airstrikes in south Lebanon and the Bekaa valley that killed at least 18 peopleShare. — Photograph: Zohra Bensemra/Reuters

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Moscow, Russia — A woman walks outside a shopping mall as black smoke rises from Gazprom Neft’s oil refinery in the south-eastern outskirts of Moscow. Ukrainian drones hit several locations across the city in Kyiv’s biggest air raid on the capital since the start of Russia’s full-scale invasion, setting a major ⁠oil refinery on fire and forcing evacuations at the country’s largest airportShare. — Photograph: AFP/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Kyiv, Ukraine — A woman reacts as she looks at residential houses damaged after a Russian strike on Kyiv. Five people were killed on Sunday night in the city when waves of drones and missiles drove residents to underground shelters and heavy explosions echoed throughout the capitalShare. — Photograph: Evgeniy Maloletka/AP

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Kyiv, Ukraine — Firefighters attempt to put out a fire at the Dormition Cathedral in the Pechersk Lavra monastery complex, a Unesco world heritage site and one of Ukraine’s most significant religious and cultural sites, following a Russian strikeShare. — Photograph: Evgeniy Maloletka/AP

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Kyiv, Ukraine — Firefighters try to put out a fire at a city marketplace following a Russian missile and drone attack in Kyiv. Before the attack, amid evidence of mounting battlefield setbacks for Russia, President Vladimir Putin had warned that Moscow would target Ukraine with ‘systemic’ strikesShare. — Photograph: Dan Bashakov/AP

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.description:

The choir does not sing in the traditional sense, but rather shouts, roars, or calls out.
